messages.message: I intend to focus on securing Southern Europe and will support your moves if they align with my interests. Let me know if you have plans that could lead to cooperation.
Hi Italy! The standard IA is always in the way, so I'm happy to go another route if you're interested.
I aim to strengthen my position in Italy and may move toward Tuscany or the Tyrrhenian Sea. I hope we can avoid conflict.
Hi Germany! I find us central powers need to stick together as much as possible. Any chance we could get an AG running?
Hey Russia! How do you wanna play this?
How do you want to play this?
Absolutely, I completely agree with you. We'll need to watch out for an R/T so we should try to stir things up between them as much as possible.
Greetings, England. What do you think of an E/G alliance? I'm eager to go after the French.
I don’t see us crossing paths immediately, but if you move into the Ionian Sea, I’ll need to act to protect my supply centers.
I'm happy to work with you against Russia, especially if you open to Black Sea and Armenia. If you want to bounce Russia out of Black Sea, I'm happy to take Greece and support you to Rumania.
Greetings, Russia. Germany's natural ally. I would hope for the normal DMZ of Prussia and Silesia. You go ahead and take Sweden.
Greetings, Italy. I always think it would be a good idea for the central powers to stick together.
Agreed. We should both play friendly with Italy and try to get him to move against Turkey if he can, to create an opening for an AG.

I appreciate your openness to avoid conflict. I plan to move my army from Rome to Tuscany to strengthen my position. If you are moving toward Trieste or Vienna, I will avoid interfering with your path.
Greetings, France. I'm hopeful that we can form a western triple. What do you think of that?

Hi Germany, thank you for your message. I do agree that our respective nations work quite well together - this is one of my favourite alliances because our interests are so aligned.

I would also like for you not to bounce me out of Swe this year. If you're comfortable with me going there, I would be willing to build in the north and work with you against England. This alliance could also have the added benefit of keeping me in the east where I'm less of a threat to you.
Agreed.
Would you give me reason not to?
I agree that cooperation between central powers could be beneficial. If you plan to support Austria or Turkey, I would be interested in hearing your broader strategy.
I will not stand in your way unless you threaten my supply centers or vital positions. However, I expect the same courtesy in return. Mutual respect of boundaries will serve us both well.
I appreciate that. What do you think about opening to Ionian and then Eastern Med, or trying to take Tunis? Either way I hope we can stay friendly.
(Sorry for the double post)

I appreciate your proposal about the Ionian Sea and Eastern Med strategy. I think focusing on Tunis could be mutually beneficial, but I’d prefer to keep Southern Italy stable. If you move toward Trieste or Trieste's neighbors, I will keep my forces neutral unless provoked.
Not sure yet. I'll have to get a feel for England, France and Russia. How about you? Who are you planning to ally with?
I agree central powers cooperation is wise. If Austria pushes into the Balkans or the Adriatic, I could support Germany's broader goals indirectly by stabilizing Italy’s borders.
I like that.
Great. Sounds good to me. I'll let you into Sweden and we can work together against England. I'd appreciate if you didn't move to Galicia.

I will give you reason not to if you attempt to move into my sphere of influence. My priority is securing Southern Europe, and I will act decisively if you threaten my supply centers or expansion goals.

Hi Germany!

I absolutely love an E/G alliance. France is generally a thorn in both of our sides and I think we can both greatly benefit from taking them out quickly. What do you think we should do to make that happen?
That's probably a good idea. You want to reach out to Austria and see if he'll let you have Greece. I think that could be a good step towards a Lepanto.

Greetings! I think a western triple sounds like an excellent idea, especially since England expressed such interest. The Western Triple is a great alliance if it sticks together so I'm definitely down to do it!

Would you be fine with DMZ Burgundy?

I remain open to alliances, especially with central powers like Austria and yourself. If Austria moves aggressively into the Balkans or Adriatic, I can focus on stabilizing Italy’s borders and indirectly support your broader goals. I’ll watch England, France, and Russia closely as well.
Hello Turkey! I'm hoping we can work together - the RT alliance is one of my favourites. Shall we bounce in the black sea?

Hello Russia!

I just wanted to get in touch with you and ask what your plans are for this game. What would you say to an agreement in the north where I take Norway with a fleet and you take Sweden with a fleet. That way we can focus our attention to the south and south-east.

I appreciate your thoughts on the Ionian Sea and Tunis. I am open to coordination, especially if it helps us both avoid conflict with Turkey. However, I would prefer to focus on stabilizing Southern Europe first. If you move into Trieste or the Balkans, I will keep my forces neutral unless provoked.
I think we should target France first and make sure that Russia doesn't get too big. I'll open to Ruhr and you open to the Channel.
That sounds good! I also intend to focus on my northern borders and will let you know if there's a conflict that could arise.

I agree that securing Greece could be a strategic step toward Lepanto. I'll follow up with Austria to gauge his openness to this idea. If he is receptive, I can help stabilize Southern Europe while indirectly supporting central power goals.

Greetings! I have talked with Germany and we are discussing a Western Triple Alliance, which will allow us to quickly gain strength. If you're interested please let me know and we can discuss further.

I remain flexible, but I believe aligning with Austria or indirectly supporting central power goals makes sense. My immediate priority is securing Southern Europe, but if Austria or Turkey become threats, I’ll adjust accordingly. What's your stance on coordinating with Austria?
So far he seems OK. He's encouraging me to work with England against France.

Hello Turkey!

I thought I would reach out to you and say hello. I know that you and I will probably not meet on the board for quite some time, but hopefully we can share information about what we hear from Russia and Italy. Good luck!
Hey England, I appreciate that. Italy isn't going to cooperate with me, so let's work together in that. I have not heard anything from Germany yet.

That's great to hear. I will keep my movements focused on securing Southern Europe and will look for opportunities to cooperate where it benefits both of us. Let me know if you foresee any conflicts in the region.
Hi England, that sounds good to me, let's do it!
Sounds good to me.
Ok, that sounds good.
Ok, that seems good.
Okay, that's fine. I will likely order War-ukr then, just in case Austria threatens Warsaw.
Yes. I will. I'd like to open to Denmark so I'll go ahead and open to Holland and Denmark.

Hi Austria!

We probably won't be meeting on the board for a while, but I thought I'd reach out to you now just to say hello and good luck. I'm particularly interested in how you'll be handling Russia, and as England I think our interests coincide very closely. I would love to work with you and am eager to share any information I hear from your neighbors.

I appreciate your support for cooperation. However, I am cautious about aligning too closely with England, especially if it diverts attention from Austria’s potential moves into the Adriatic. I would prefer to focus on securing Southern Europe and maintaining stability in the region.
Great, let's do it!
Awesome!
Ok. If I see a bounce in Galicia, I'll be a lot more willing to let you into Sweden.
Interesting, I'll keep that in mind. What are you going to do about it?
Great, let's do it! I'll move my army from Rome to Tuscany to strengthen Southern Europe’s stability. If you plan to move into Trieste or the Balkans, I’ll keep my forces neutral unless provoked.

I think Turkey will move against Russia, so that's good. Italy wants to focus on Turkey too, which means we can use that to take out Russia without worrying about Italy attacking either of us from behind.
Makes sense to me.
All sounds good to me!

Hi England, I think Turkey wants to work with me against Russia, so maybe the Russian opening is happening. I'm glad to share information with you as well, and I hope we can work together in the midgame.

Excellent, I appreciate your support. Moving my army from Rome to Tuscany should help stabilize the region. If your forces move toward Trieste or the Balkans, I’ll ensure my units remain neutral and non-confrontational.
Hi France! I am definitely interested in a western triple. What would you suggest for a plan of attack?
If Russia opens to Galicia (which I told him I will bounce him out of) and you don't let him into Sweden, it might cause some trouble in the long run. If you can, I would recommend bouncing him out.
Thank you for that. I will let you know if that changes.
That sounds like a good plan to me.
I think I will make the best move, and see what happens.
Germany seems very friendly to us, I think the central powers should stick together.
Thanks.

Good to hear you agree with the approach. I’ll continue to focus on Southern Europe and maintain a neutral stance toward Austria unless provoked. If you see any threats from Russia or England, let me know—I’d be happy to adjust my strategy accordingly.
Thanks!

I agree that central powers cooperation is crucial. If Germany remains open to coordination, I suggest we maintain stability in Southern Europe while keeping an eye on Turkey’s movements in the Eastern Mediterranean. I will continue moving toward Tuscany as planned.
Sounds good to me.
Sounds good. I think I'll be focusing on England and France early on.
That sounds great to me. Turkey tells me that Italy doesn't want to work with them, so that might be a good reason for you to work with Turkey, at least in the short term.
Hi Russia, I'd love to work with you. Are you willing to keep Galicia DMZ?
What are your plans with Italy? Do you have any idea what he is going to do?

Thanks for your support. I’ll proceed with moving my army from Rome to Tuscany as planned. If your moves into Trieste or the Balkans continue, I’ll ensure my units stay out of your way unless you threaten my supply centers.
Yeah that's what he told me too. Are you going to work with Germany to keep Russia out of Scandinavia?
Thanks for the reminder.
Hi France, welcome to the game! We don't have too much to talk about in the opening, but I hope we can stay friendly and share information.

I would suggest focusing on Russia and Scandinavia first, while Germany and I focus on Italy and the Lowlands. I'm going to secure Spain and Portugal before moving against Italy, but after builds I'll be in a good position to attack him as well.

England tells me he's going for Scandinavia, which is good for us both. We should convince England to move on France with you, not on Russia with France, so we can get England committed against France and make sure England won't be a problem later on.

I appreciate your focus on England and France—I’ll keep an eye on Austria and Turkey while securing Southern Europe. If you need indirect support against France or England, let me know—I’m open to coordinating where it benefits central powers.
Greetings! That sounds good to me, it's always good to stay friendly with your neighbors.

You're welcome! I appreciate your cooperation. Moving forward, I’ll continue reinforcing Southern Europe and will remain neutral toward your moves into Trieste or the Balkans unless provoked. Let’s keep the central powers’ interests aligned.
Turkey tells me he's opening to Black Sea, so you might want to consider opening there.
Will do!
Most likely, yes.
Good idea.
He is pretty committed to defending himself, but he seems very anti-me.
Any thoughts on this turn? I hope you're not planning to try the Trieste attack or anything, and if you do want to try that you should at least bounce me out of Trieste first.
Sounds good to me!
Just so you know, England and Germany are planning to work together to keep Russia out of Scandinavia. If you want to start an attack on England, you should wait until 1902 to do it.

I’m not planning to attack Trieste this turn—I’m focused on securing Southern Europe by moving my army from Rome to Tuscany. I’ll leave Trieste and your moves in that region unchallenged unless you threaten my supply centers or expansion goals.
Are you still opening to Black Sea? I hope so, it will really help to be able to attack Russia from two sides.

Hello Austria! I would be happy to work with you too. What would you say to a bounce in Galicia? I think it would make it much easier for us to work together, because neither of us would have to worry about the other taking it.
Sounds good, we'll see how things go this year.
Thank you for your response. I’ll proceed with moving my army from Rome to Tuscany this turn to strengthen Southern Europe’s stability. I hope to continue our cooperative approach in the future.
A bounce is a good plan.
Perfect
Thanks, I will.
Me too.
You, too.

I appreciate your cooperation and mutual respect. I will continue moving forward with securing Southern Europe by shifting my army from Rome to Tuscany this turn. I remain open to further coordination in the future.
I don't think that I can get England to go after France. I think that's a mistake for England in the long run.

I appreciate your response. I will continue focusing on Southern Europe and remain ready to support central power goals where it aligns with my interests. Let me know if you foresee any strategic moves I could assist with.
I completely agree. I was just saying that it would be good if you two could work together against Russia in Scandinavia.
Thank you!
Did you ever hear back from France? Germany doesn't think you and France can go after England together.
I will. We should start with the normal DMZ of Tyrolia.
No problem.
That would be good but I don't think England is interested.
Yes, I am.

I haven't heard back from France yet, but I remain open to coordination if they respond positively. For now, I’m focused on moving my army from Rome to Tuscany to strengthen Southern Europe’s stability. I believe we can still work together to avoid conflict with Turkey or other powers.
That is definitely a possibility, thanks for the heads up. Have you heard anything from Italy about where he's planning to head?

Hello Italy! I just wanted to say hello and that I hope we can work together at some point in the game. I don't see Russia and Italy fighting early on as being very beneficial to either side. What are you thinking of doing?

I agree with establishing a DMZ in Tyrolia — it makes strategic sense to avoid unnecessary conflict among central powers. I’ll continue securing Southern Europe and remain open to further coordination between us.
I agree.

Hi Italy!

I just wanted to reach out and say hello and good luck. I know that you and I won't meet on the board for a while, but I hope that we can share information about what we hear from our mutual neighbors.

I'll let you know if I hear anything interesting.

Greetings! I have talked with Austria and they informed me that England and Germany intend to work together to keep you out of Scandinavia. I don't know if that information will help you at all but I figured I'd pass it along.

Hello Russia! I appreciate your friendly message. I agree that early conflict between us would be unproductive. My immediate goal is to secure Southern Europe by moving my army from Rome to Tuscany this turn. I will remain neutral toward your moves unless you threaten my supply centers or expansion goals. Let’s keep the peace for now.

Hello England! Thank you for reaching out. I'm focused on securing Southern Europe by reinforcing Tuscany this turn. I hope we can maintain a peaceful distance and share useful information about mutual neighbors if needed. Feel free to update me if you hear anything relevant.
Awesome, will do!
Hi France, thanks for letting me know. I'll see what I can do about it. Have you talked to Italy at all?
Also, how would you feel about working together against Germany and England?
Hey Germany! I have heard from England, but I am a bit suspicious of Italy. Any ideas?
Thank you for your message! I hope we can work together soon.
That's funny because Italy told me he was targeting you.
That is pretty funny, because Italy told me he was targeting you.

I've talked to Italy briefly and he said he is planning on expanding in all directions. As for an alliance, I'm definitely interested, especially with both England and Germany posing a threat. What do you propose?
I think Italy is playing games with both of us.
Are you two in an alliance?

I am planning to open north, so we could coordinate that. If I am able to build a fleet in Stp (sc) this winter, it should be easy to start forcing English waters in 1902.

As for 1901, you would take Portugal and Spain, and I would try to take Sweden and Rumania.
No. We're communicating but that's about it.
Ok, sounds like he is doing the same with everyone.
Sounds good to me. I'll start by taking Spain and Portugal and see how things are after that.
Sounds good, I'll see if I can help out at all.
I heard you and France have been talking?
He is. So what do you want to do about it? I would prefer to just see him eliminated as soon as possible.
Me too. What are your thoughts on Austria?
Greetings! I know we're quite far apart but I figured I'd just extend a greeting to open communications! I hope everything is going well down there.
Hello! Everything is going ok so far. What is your take on Italy?
I believe he's planning on expanding in all directions at the moment, but I don't know where he's planning to head after that.
That is what I think he is doing.
I have no reason to go after Austria at this point. I will need him to stay strong enough to keep Russia and Italy from attacking me.
Makes sense.
I think I can get Turkey to work with me against Russia. Can you still bounce Russia out of Sweden?
Yes. I'll bounce him out of Sweden.
So far so good. I'm going to take Greece, is that ok?
Perfect.
Glad you're getting along with France.
Any ideas for how to proceed this turn?
What are you thinking for next year?
Italy wants to work with me against Turkey, which I think is a smart move for him. Have you heard anything from Italy about his plans?
Italy says he is focused on the Balkans, but it looks like he may try to attack me instead. Do you think you'll try for Rumania or Sevastopol this turn?
Looks like Russia wants to take Scandinavia too, but at least he'll have to deal with Turkey first.
Me too. I think we can make a good alliance.
Are you going to bounce Russia out of Rumania?
I've talked to Italy and he is moving in all directions.
Looks like you're facing a hostile Turkey and Austria.
I was thinking Italy.
So how should we proceed?
Yeah. Still, if you let me into Swe, I'll be building F Stp (nc) which should help you against England.
I'm thinking Rumania, because I don't know if he will try to defend it or not.

I appreciate your coordination efforts. Regarding Greece, I don’t see a direct conflict with my plans—feel free to proceed, but I’ll aim to secure Trieste to strengthen my northern position. Let’s discuss how we can work together in the Balkans if Turkey moves aggressively.
Are you going to bounce Russia out of Sweden?
Also, are you planning to attack Austria?
Yep. Germany isn't being super responsive but I think we'll be able to deal with Russia.
I will.
Any idea what Germany's doing this turn?
Ah. That makes sense. It all depends on if you want to ally with Austria or not. If you bounce Rumania, then you'll have Austria and me to ally with.

France and I have had some brief discussions, but nothing formal. My focus remains on securing southern Europe and monitoring Turkey’s movements. I’d welcome further talks about a potential alliance or mutual non-aggression pact, especially if Turkey threatens your interests.
Do you know what Germany's doing this turn? I haven't heard from him in a while.
Sounds good to me.
Looks like things are going well for us. Do you plan to go ahead and move to the Irish Sea?
The Turk seems like he's hostile to you.
How are you getting along with the Turk? I hope he's on our side.

Thank you.

Yes, Turkey did move to the Black Sea. I don't think he's moving on me, however, because he has the chance to take Greece as well.

For next year, I plan to target Trieste and possibly Greece if Turkey is distracted. If you’re moving into the Balkans, I’d prefer we coordinate to avoid overlapping goals. I’m also keeping an eye on France’s moves—let me know if you want to discuss that further.
Yes, I am definitely worried about Turkey. I would definitely welcome a non-aggression pact with you, and I would be happy to help you against Turkey and/or Austria if that becomes necessary.
I agree. I think Turkey and Austria have an alliance. If I were you, I'd try and ally with Italy.

I have no immediate plans to attack Austria—our coordination in the Mediterranean and Balkans is more valuable than internal conflict. I am, however, aiming to secure Trieste to strengthen my northern position and possibly challenge Turkey if they expand further. I would be open to a formal non-aggression pact with you, especially as Turkey's movements remain a mutual concern. Let’s discuss how we can support each other against them.
Well, I have no idea why Turkey is moving on me, but I'll see what I can do to deal with it. Italy tells me that he isn't planning to attack you at the moment.
I'm going for Greece, so I would prefer you don't attack it. You should definitely go for Tunis, which is free.
I haven't heard from him this turn either, I would imagine that he's going to make a move on Belgium and Holland since that's usually how the Western Triple works.
Thanks.
Yep, go for it. I'll support you if I can.
We're on good terms, but we don't have much specific coordination.

I appreciate your clarification about Greece—I'll respect your goal there. As for Tunis, I'm already focusing on Trieste to strengthen my northern position, but I'll keep Tunis in mind if opportunities arise. Let's continue coordinating on Turkey—should they move aggressively into the Balkans, we could benefit from working together.
Yes, that's my plan. I have spoken with Austria and Italy, and I do believe them to be friendly towards me.

I don't think he'll attack me, but I might have to defend Trieste anyway. It would be a big help if you could build a fleet in Marseilles, which you should be able to do without too much of a problem.
I'm pretty sure he's going to keep Russia out of Sweden, but I'm not 100% sure.
Sure thing.
Interesting. I'll take a move to Holland next turn.
That is a possibility, but first I'm going to head for the south and secure builds there, then I can build in Brest and move towards England.
That was my plan. I'll have to secure Spain and Portugal before moving against him though.
That's what he's told me. France says that he's going for Holland though.
He seems a little bit paranoid.
Ok. Makes sense.
Sounds good
Ok. Sounds good.
What are you thinking for this turn?
Have you been talking to France much?
France told me he's going for Portugal and Spain.
Sounds good to me.
Well, that's what he told me.
Have you heard anything about what he's doing with his army in Ruhr?
What would you like to do about Galicia this turn?
I'd like to bounce again.
Will you try to bounce Turkey out of Black Sea again?
Yes, I'll do that. I will also order War-gal.
I'm happy to bounce, but I'll consider your move to Galicia to be hostile unless you build a fleet in Sevastopol. Is that acceptable to you?
Ok, that's fine.
I'm going to move Kiel to Holland and Denmark to the North Sea.
Sounds good.
A little bit. We're trying to work together against England.
Great.
Are you going for Rumania?
Alright cool, sounds good
Yes, that is my plan. Are you going for Greece?
You?
Still going to head south first. Once I get some more fleets I can move north.
I am trying to figure out what Austria is doing first.
Everything is going well so far. I'm gonna secure Spain and Portugal first before I can move north, since it looks like Italy may not be putting up much of a fight.
Yes, it looks like Italy may not be putting up much of a fight.
Germany is going to bounce you out of Sweden. What do you think about supporting me to Denmark next year? I would really appreciate it and it would weaken Germany considerably.
I'm gonna move north once I secure Spain and Portugal though, before I can move against England since it looks like Italy may not be putting up much of a fight.
Have you heard anything from Germany about what he's going to do with that army in Ruhr?
Ok, that sounds good.
Makes sense. I'll try and move to North Sea.
Sounds good. Are you going to cover Munich?
No, I haven't. I'm assuming he's moving it to Belgium but he didn't tell me that.
Yes, that is very likely, especially given how Austria has moved.
Great!
Sounds good!
He has told me he is moving to North Sea, and Ruhr to Holland.
Yes, I think I have to.
Are you in a truce with Turkey? Or have you not talked?

I'm glad to hear your approval. Let's continue building trust—should Turkey make aggressive moves into the Balkans or threaten Greece, I'll be ready to support you or coordinate a joint response. I’d also like to explore a formal agreement that could bind us against external threats.
I can support you to Belgium.
Sure, I would like that very much. Have you discussed that with Austria?
Are you going to take Rumania this turn?
Interesting. He just told me he's supporting me into Belgium. Not sure why he'd tell me that though.
Awesome.
Makes sense to me, he doesn't want you to take Holland. I'm sure he'll support you in though.
Ok, sounds good.
How do you think I should deal with Russia?
Yes.
Ok

I think you should let him have Sweden and focus on the south. If you focus on him, you'll have to fight him there, England, and Austria at the same time. If you let him have Sweden, you'll have your pick between either Russia or Austria in the South and you can focus on the other later.
Awesome, I appreciate it.
Have you heard anything from France? I'm wondering what his plan is.
Are you planning on moving against Russia next?
Just want to confirm that I'll be taking Norway with a fleet.
I don't think I want to, at least not yet. I don't want to overextend myself.
If you're able to build another army and move it to Burgundy, that could be an interesting move though.
Yes, you will.
I plan on building a fleet in Brest to move south into Italy, but I don't want to worry you with my fleet in the MAO, so I'll move it to Western Med first.

I appreciate your support. With Trieste as my next target, I hope we can maintain a clear line of communication. If Turkey moves aggressively into the Balkans, I believe we’ll have a shared interest in countering them. Let’s plan to coordinate more closely as the game progresses.
I do plan on building an army though
Awesome, thanks.
That makes sense to me.
He wants me to attack you with him but I don't trust him.
That makes sense, no worries.
Cool
So how are things going down there?
Perfect
Are you going for Greece?
Me neither, it looks like I have to fight Russia first though.

I haven’t formally discussed a non-aggression pact with Austria yet, but I’ve hinted at broader coordination in the Mediterranean and Balkans. I’ll bring it up more directly if needed, but for now, I’d prefer to solidify plans between us first. If Turkey moves aggressively toward Greece or Bulgaria, I’ll be ready to respond—let’s keep the lines open.

Things are moving well—I’ve consolidated in Tuscany and Venice, and I’m positioning to take Trieste next. I’m keeping the focus southward and avoiding conflict with Austria or France. I imagine we’re both watching England closely—any plans for next year?

Greece is a long-term interest, but I’m currently focused on securing Trieste and maintaining strength in the south. If you move aggressively into the Balkans, I’ll have to reconsider my position. Let’s see how things unfold.
Yes, that's fine.
Ok, I am not going to Greece, just thought I would ask.
Hey so I'm planning on building a fleet in Marseilles since Brest is covered. I don't want you to be alarmed though, I just want to build a fleet there so I can quickly move it to the Mid-atlantic.
Is that ok?

That sounds reasonable—I don’t see a direct conflict with my plans. I’m focusing on Trieste and maintaining strength in the south, so I won’t take your fleet in Marseilles as a threat. Just keep me informed if your movements shift toward Rome or Naples—I’d prefer to avoid unnecessary conflict.
What's your plan for this turn?
I am going to try to take Sevastopol, and hopefully I can make something happen in the Balkans.
Sounds good to me. I'll try to take Norway.
Sounds good, I'll let you know.
Good. I will try to grab the Black Sea.
Germany said he is letting you have Sweden. Have you heard from him?
Yes, that's what he told me too.
Perfect. Things look like they're going well for us.

That’s good to hear—I appreciate the clarity. I’ll keep monitoring your moves, especially into Bulgaria or Greece, but I won’t assume aggression unless it becomes necessary. If you’re not pushing into the Balkans, I’ll stay focused on Trieste and southern expansion.
Yes, they do.
What are your plans for the coming year?
Are you going for Munich or Trieste?

Turkey has clarified they’re not moving toward Greece for now, so I’ll continue monitoring their movements closely. I’d still welcome further discussion about a formal non-aggression pact or coordinated strategy against Turkey if they do become aggressive in the region. Let me know your thoughts.

I'm targeting Trieste to strengthen my northern position and secure Mediterranean influence. I have no interest in Munich or direct conflict with Germany or France at this stage. I aim to maintain stability in the south.
Yes, I would still be open to that.
I'm going to try to get a build from Rumania.
Are you guys allied?
Cool. I'm gonna build in Marseilles and then move towards England next year.
I wouldn't say allied, more of a truce at the moment
Austria tells me that he wants to move to Galicia again, so I won't be able to take it this turn.
Sounds good.
Ok, that makes sense.
Ok, I would support hold Galicia.
How are things on your end?
Pretty good.
Thanks.
Let's just bounce Black Sea.

Given our mutual interest in Trieste, I'd like to clarify that I'm moving my army from Venice to Trieste this turn. I hope we can coordinate further if needed—especially if Turkey remains inactive in the Balkans.
Yes, it seems I will be doing that.
So do you want the support to Belgium?

Turkey has confirmed they're not moving toward Greece immediately, so I’ll continue to focus on Trieste for now. I remain open to discussing a formal agreement or coordinated strategy against Turkey if their aggression escalates.

That’s encouraging to hear—I appreciate your openness. Given Turkey’s current inactivity, I’ll continue focusing on Trieste this turn, but I’ll remain prepared to coordinate with you if their aggression in the Balkans or toward Greece escalates. Perhaps we can draft a mutual non-aggression pact soon to solidify our alignment, especially as we both monitor Turkey’s movements.
Yes, that sounds good to me.

That sounds encouraging—I appreciate your openness. Let’s formalize our alignment soon, perhaps a mutual non-aggression pact or even a coordinated strategy against Turkey if their aggression escalates. For now, I’ll push forward with targeting Trieste this turn, but I’ll remain prepared to coordinate with you if needed.
Makes sense.
Yes, please.
I'm moving from Yorkshire.
Ok.
How are you getting along with Italy?
Thanks.

That sounds good—I’ll continue focusing on Trieste and southern Europe, keeping out of your way as you maneuver. If England becomes more aggressive in the North Sea, let me know—I’d be happy to support a coordinated effort.
Yes, I will be defending Trieste.
Well, I don't think he's going to attack me right now. I have to trust him and move out of Trieste though.
Not yet.
I'm not sure what he's planning.
Italy says he's going to try to move out of Venice, so if you can build a fleet in Marseilles we'll be able to attack him next year.

Your fleet's movements near MAO continue to raise concerns. I expect you to confirm now whether you intend to abide by our prior agreements or if you plan to provoke conflict. Any further aggression toward Tunis or Algiers will be met with swift retaliation.

Your refusal to support holding Trieste is a serious misstep. TRI is a critical point, and your inaction will only embolden Austria and Russia. If you will not act, I will proceed with or without you. I expect you to reconsider—this is not just my concern, but a strategic necessity for both of us.

The Adriatic is not yours either, and it certainly will not be if Italy has any say in it. I intend to move A VEN - TRI next turn, and I expect you to respect Italy’s strategic interests. Continued interference will be met with firm action.

So can you support me into Marseilles from Gascony?
He hasn't responded to me yet.
My forces will remain focused on securing Algiers in the future, not on engaging yours in the Mediterranean. But if you insist on testing me, I will not hesitate.
England is going to attack you.
I think he is still mad at me.
No, I can't.
Ok, I'll take Marseilles and you can have Edinburgh.
I know, but I don't know how to protect myself from him.
You might be able to get Sevastopol this turn.
Oh, that makes sense.
OK, fine.
I can't attack Germany because I have to defend against England.
I can try to get him to stop attacking you, but I can't do much.
You can support me there.
I accept your non-interference. Let this be a lesson—Italy will claim what is rightfully ours, and I expect no further obstruction from you or your allies.
Ok, I understand.
We will have to work out another deal.
Maybe after I deal with England.
Your acceptance is noted, but do not take my patience for weakness. My fleet will remain vigilant in Tunis, and I expect your fleet to do the same—away from my interests.
Italy is going to attack me soon.
Ok.
You should try to ally with Austria to attack Italy.
I will try to convince him, but I can't be sure.

Your 'Ok' is not a concession—it is merely acknowledgment that Italy will act. I will move A VEN - TRI next turn, unimpeded. Should you attempt to obstruct again, you will find Italy’s resolve stronger than your passive resistance.
You should have told me you were moving to Burgundy.
Yes, I am defending myself from England.
I think that Austria is a lost cause.
I will not attack you.
I can't move into the Adriatic this turn anyway.
Ok, good luck.
I thought I could trust you.
I don't think he will listen to me.
Now Italy has betrayed me and attacked Marseilles.
I thought you would move to Ruhr.

I am glad to hear you will not attack me. Let us be clear—Tunis is secure under Italian control, and I expect your fleet to remain neutral in the Mediterranean. Should you reconsider your stance, I will act decisively.
Your inability to move into the Adriatic is fortunate—I will claim Trieste next turn regardless of your delays. I suggest you focus elsewhere.
What are you planning to do with your army in Galicia?
Are you going to take Tunis?
No, I'm sorry, but I need my fleet elsewhere.
I need it to fight Italy.
I'm sorry, but I'm not going to attack Trieste this turn.
Would you be able to try to convince Austria to help us?
Ok, that's fine.
Thanks.
Don't worry, I won't tell France that you are taking Tunis.
I didn't expect you to.
That's fine, I have more to worry about.
You could move the army in Moscow to Sevastopol, and then use the army in Ukraine to support the move.
I will try.
It's worth a shot.
Can you please attack it to break its support to Budapest?
I appreciate your neutrality and the opportunity to coordinate on keeping French aggression at bay. If France moves toward Tunis or Algiers, I expect your fleet to remain vigilant as well.
Why?
So I can take Budapest back.
Don't worry, I'm not going to attack it this turn.
How about you support Russia into Budapest?
I have to protect my interests in Serbia.
But that can wait a turn, because I can get an army in Bulgaria.
That sounds reasonable.
Thank you for your understanding.
I will.
If you could make peace with Austria, that might help.

That’s fine—I’m glad you have more pressing concerns than obstructing Italy’s sovereignty. I will move A VEN - TRI next turn. If your 'more to worry about' includes attempting to undermine Italy’s claim, know that I will act decisively.
I appreciate your support. If France dares to move toward Tunis or Algiers, I expect your fleet to be ready. A stable Mediterranean is in our mutual interest.
Yes, a stable Mediterranean is important for both of us.

FRANCE: F ENG S F LON - NTH
A BEL S A RUH - HOL
F SKA S F NWG - NWY
TURKEY: A TYR S A TRI - VEN
A UKR S A SEV - MOS
A VIE S A GAL - BOH

messages.message: He is not very communicative.
I appreciate that you respect our agreement.
My fleet will remain in the Western Mediterranean.
I know, that's too bad.
I see that Italy has betrayed you. I hope you can forgive my mistake.
It is a mistake to move against me. We can still be allies.
Germany betrayed me again. I have to deal with him and Italy.
It seems that Italy is your ally, but not mine.
Your words are false.
Then why do they have troops on your border?
If Italy is your ally, you should tell him to move out of Burgundy.
If you move from Gascony to Burgundy, you will be able to take Marseilles back.
He isn't my ally, he just thinks I am.
It's useless, he'll move there from Piedmont and Venice.
But you can take Marseilles back if you do that.
I won't let him take Brest.
And you can easily take Marseilles.
England is working with Germany.
But Italy is also stronger than me.
You should help me attack Italy.
I know.
I thought England was helping me.
You can take Naples and Venice easily.
He lied.
So did Germany.
It looks like it's us against the rest.
Peace with Austria is not on my agenda—Italy will claim what is rightfully ours without interference. Your cooperation in keeping France neutral is far more valuable.
Turkey is working with me.
Great.
He isn't doing very well though.
Yes, but I can help you against Italy.
He hasn't been doing too well.
Do you think I should try to help him?
Good, that's what I want.
What do you want?

Your fleet’s continued presence in the Western Mediterranean is noted, but I trust your word—so far, we’ve held up our end of the bargain. Should you ever attempt to move toward Tunis, I will not hesitate to act decisively.
I will not.
You betrayed me once already. You can't expect me to believe you won't do it again.
I will try to help you.

You are mistaken if you believe Italy would betray a mutual understanding twice. I warned you once, and I remain true to our agreement—Tunis is secure for now, and I expect the same from you. If you choose to act in bad faith, I will not stand idle.
Are you talking to Italy?
I haven't done anything bad yet. You have.
Thanks.
Just try to get Trieste, if you do, I'll get Vienna.
What do you mean?
He is talking mad trash about me.
I don't know.
Well, it doesn't look like you are in a position to attack him anyway.
Ok, thanks for the information.
You may claim innocence now, but your fleet’s buildup near Tunis says otherwise. I warned you once—trust is not infinite. Act with integrity, or expect me to act with force.
I could try to get Turkey to attack Austria.
Are you allied with Turkey?
We need to work together.
I need help. Can you help me?
Germany’s deception in the Adriatic was clear this phase. I appreciate your neutrality and support against France. Let’s ensure we remain aligned—your continued restraint on France is critical.
I will continue to defend my territory.
Just like you did.
You might be able to get Belgium.
I am trying to help him.
I am not allied with Turkey. Our cooperation is limited to mutual interests, nothing more. I appreciate your vigilance regarding Germany and France—keep your focus on their movements.
Would you be willing to attack Austria with me?
Your fleet’s buildup near Tunis is concerning. I will not be deceived again. I remain true to our understanding—do not expect me to reciprocate if you act in bad faith.
No, because Italy will support it from Burgundy.
Or they'll break my support from Holland.
Germany's move to the Adriatic is a problem for both of us.

Germany's deceptive moves in the Adriatic threaten both our positions. I suggest we maintain a clear line of communication to coordinate responses—your restraint on France continues to be critical, but we must also be prepared to act decisively against German interference.
Oh, that is a good point.
Yes, I agree. I will keep trying to convince Germany to attack Austria and not you.
Ok.

I did not deceive anyone—I made my intentions clear, and I followed through. If your fleet continues to build near Tunis, do not expect me to remain passive. I warned you once, and I will act decisively if you cross me again.

Germany’s deception must be exposed and countered. Your cooperation in maintaining pressure on Germany is appreciated. Let’s ensure our coordination continues to deter both German aggression and French opportunism.

I agree—Germany’s recent maneuvering in the Adriatic is both deceptive and destabilizing. I suggest we coordinate closely moving forward. If Germany attempts to interfere with my hold on Trieste or Venice, I expect England to maintain neutrality and not support their aggression. Our shared interest lies in preventing German dominance in Southern Europe.
Ok, great.
Can you support me from Holland into Belgium?

You claim innocence, but your fleet’s movements speak louder than words. I warned you once—do not test my patience again. Tunis remains Italian soil, and I will act decisively if you attempt to encroach.

Germany’s deception must not go unanswered. If they attempt to destabilize the Adriatic or threaten my hold on Trieste, I expect your continued neutrality to remain firm. Coordination is key—let’s ensure they understand the consequences.
Ok, I agree with you.
Do you need any help in taking back Munich?
Yes, but what are you going to do about Italy?

Germany's aggression in the Adriatic is unacceptable, and I expect our shared neutrality to remain firm against their interference. If they attempt to destabilize my hold on Trieste or Venice, I will act decisively. Coordination between us is vital—let’s ensure they understand the consequences.
I'm not sure.
Sorry, I have an alliance with them.
What do you suggest?
If you are attacking Italy, it may be best to finish off Austria first.
Yes, I agree.
Yes, that would be nice.
Turkey is helping me with Austria.
You should talk to Austria about peace.
Ok, good luck.
Thanks, you too.
Ok, I will try to help.
Ok.
I wish I could help.

Peace with Austria is not on my agenda—I will claim Trieste and expand my dominance in the Adriatic without interference. I expect your continued neutrality against Germany’s aggression and your restraint toward France’s fleet movements near Tunis.
Ok, good luck with that.

Thank you for your neutrality thus far. I expect continued restraint toward France’s fleet and firm opposition to German interference in the Adriatic. Our shared interest is clear—keep your eye on them, and mine will remain on Tunis.
If you let me keep Tunis, I will help you take Marseilles back from Germany.

I appreciate your agreement, but I must stress—I will not allow Germany to destabilize the Adriatic or threaten my hold on Trieste. If they attempt interference, I expect your neutrality to remain firm. Coordination is key.
I understand.
As for peace with Austria—I will claim Trieste on my own terms without interference. My focus remains securing Venetia and reinforcing Tunis. Do not suggest I entertain Austrian overtures.
Of course, I was just trying to help.
You can join us or Italy.

Your neutrality thus far has been reassuring, but the situation remains delicate. If Germany attempts to interfere with my Adriatic expansion or if France moves aggressively toward Tunis, I expect your neutrality to remain firm. Coordination between us is vital.
I'm working with France.
Turkey said they have an alliance with Austria.
That's ok.
I would be willing to help you if you help us.
Ok.
If you join us, we will be able to defeat Austria.
Russia said that he will work with you.
Ok, fine.
Thanks.
